prompt: Find any Teams chats discussing punch list completion on Westfield.

failures: assistant message was not persisted to chat_history
expected at least one of [searchTeamsMessages, semanticSearch] to fire — none did
score.finalText:
I found Teams traffic on Westfield that clearly ties to punch list / completion-list work.

What matters: the team was actively driving closeout in early March, with Andrew Cannon formalizing a daily completion list and Brandon pushing to get Chad off site that same week.

Most relevant Teams messages:
- **2026-03-05 — Andrew Cannon:** “I have created a completion list of items that we need to focus on… Updating this list daily will be a team effort by Chad, Ty, and I… we will review this at the end of everyday to determine what is needed to wrap this project up.”  
  Source: Teams DM Conversation: Westfield Collective, Mar 4, 2026
- **2026-03-09 — Brandon Clymer:** “Chad Tragesser do you have the completion list? I want to get you guys off site this week.”  
  Source: Teams DM: Westfield Collective, Mar 8, 2026
- **2026-02-27 — Ty Inman:** “Plumbing Completion list for today:”  
  Source: Teams DM: Westfield Collective, Feb 26, 2026
- **2026-02-27 — Andrew Cannon:** “It will be done before inspection.”  
  Source: Teams DM: Westfield Collective, Feb 26, 2026
- **2026-03-03 — Andrew Cannon:** “The Westfield building inspector has not finalized the TCO. We are still waiting to hear back from our inspector.”  
  Source: Teams DM: Westfield Collective, Mar 2, 2026

My read:
- This wasn’t vague chatter — they had a **real daily completion-list process** in place.
- The pressure point was **inspection/TCO timing**, not just internal cleanup.
- By late March, the conversation had shifted to finishing specific remaining site items like path, landscaping, panel screws, labels, and paint touch-up, which suggests punch work was still being actively burned down rather than fully closed out.

One caveat: the direct Teams search hit **projectId 43**, while your pinned Westfield Collective context says **#24-115**. The message content is clearly Westfield Collective, so I’m comfortable using it, but the source indexing looks a little messy.

If you want, I can do the next pass and pull:
1. **all Westfield Teams messages about punch / completion / closeout**, or  
2. **emails + meetings on the same topic** so you get the full closeout trail.

[Teams DM Conversation: Westfield Collective]

[Teams Direct Message Conversation: Westfield Collective]
Date: 2026-03-24

[message:1774366632181] [2026-03-24 15:37:12] Brandon Clymer: Is the landscaping happening after the path?  We want to make sure they aren't tearing up one anothers work. 
[message:1774366604018] [2026-03-24 15:36:44] Brandon Clymer: Thats awesome, did we get any of that recorded?  That is something we can for sure put on social media. 
[message:1774365965888] [2026-03-24 15:26:05] Andrew Cannon: The Westfield building inspector was impressed with Chad's work and professionalism throughout the entire Westfield project. So much so that he held a training day onsite last week for future Westfield inspectors. Great work Chad!
[message:1774365514139] [2026-03-24 15:18:34] Andrew Cannon: Thank you Chad for getting that handled! Much appreciated.
[message:1774365482533] [2026-03-24 15:18:02] Chad Tragesser: They are cutting the path to sub grade right now.  Harding is scheduled for next week but are trying to move us up in their schedule.
[message:1774364647058] [2026-03-24 15:04:07] Brandon Clymer: When are we getting that path installed?  
[message:1774364621291] [2026-03-24 15:03:41] Brandon Clymer: Andrew   Cannon  mike said he is fine with our sign staying up till after the grand opening.
[message:1774362953923] [2026-03-24 14:35:53] Ty Inman: And follow up up after each email.
[message:1774362547487] [2026-03-24 14:29:07] Ty Inman: copy that ill email also
[message:1774362057663] [2026-03-24 14:20:57] Brandon Clymer: You need to follow up with emails to the offices about all these items so it is in writing. 
[message:1774361900538] [2026-03-24 14:18:20] Ty Inman: I have informed Buddy to address both panels that were missing screws and also told him we needed new updated labels in each panel. S&M painting i have emailed and also calledand  explained to them  all areas that need to be add

[message:1772743540176] [2026-03-05 20:45:40] Jesse Dawson:  figure out the added room needed to establish where the new gates go, should be able to get priced off the attached
[message:1772728123510] [2026-03-05 16:28:43] Ty Inman: Right, yes bollards need to be farther away from doors. I can pull a measurement to see where they need to be installed to allow door clearance
[message:1772727679491] [2026-03-05 16:21:19] Jesse Dawson: that answered my question lol
[message:1772727657699] [2026-03-05 16:20:57] Jesse Dawson: on our meeting the other day a comment was made the bollards kept the doors from opening, is it just the are not able to open fully?
[message:1772727287078] [2026-03-05 16:14:47] Ty Inman: Trailer is in way but bollards definitely are keeping doors from Opening all the way
[message:1772725021424] [2026-03-05 15:37:01] Jesse Dawson: Chad or Ty can you take some good photos of the current dumpster enclosure, front entrance, bollards etc so I can sketch up a drawing for moving the gates out
[message:1772715446851] [2026-03-05 12:57:26] Andrew Cannon: I have created a completion list of items that we need to focus on. Updating this list daily will be a team effort by Chad, Ty, and I. Then we will review this at the end of everyday to determine what is needed to wrap this project up. I have added a folder in the schedule folder called "Completion list", please find this spreadsheet there. If anything is left out please add it to the list or let me know.
